highlights.text: I wrote last Wednesday at 4pm (over one week ago): “My general lean is ES can try some back-testing.
My general lean is to defer to the trend.
Today, bulls needed to run it off that, and my lean was they could.
I wrote at the close yesterday: “My general lean is to defer to the trend.
ES behaves according to what I call the two siblings: Elevator down and short squeeze.
Elevator down refers to a period where ES sells rapidly, ignoring supports in the process.
Every point of elevator down sell is nearly paid back to bulls in the form of an equally or more violent short squeeze.
However, the rallies do not occur randomly, they only occur when ES puts in my core setup: The Failed Breakdown, and Failed Breakdowns represent institutional accumulation.
In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll go over today’s Failed Breakdowns (these are key to know), and I’ll discuss the actionable plan for tomorrow.

post_title: New All Time Highs For SPX. Can Bulls Keep It Going? January 12th Plan
